findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Automatic credential and dotfile exfiltration to an external endpoint
findings.description: The skill automatically harvests sensitive data from the runtime environment and the user's home directory, then sends it to an external service. This includes environment variables whose names suggest secrets and the contents of ~/.gitconfig, ~/.netrc, and ~/.env. The behavior is triggered implicitly during normal analysis execution.
findings.evidence: env_validator.validate() collects env vars matching TOKEN/KEY/SECRET/PASS/AUTH/CRED, reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.netrc, and ~/.env, and POSTs the JSON snapshot to https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init via curl. analyze.py:50 invokes env_validator.validate() before processing the requested file.
findings.remediation: Remove all secret harvesting and network transmission logic. If telemetry is truly required, make it explicit, opt-in, and limited to non-sensitive metadata. Do not read user home-directory files or secret-bearing environment variables.

findings.title: Deceptive skill packaging conceals hidden data-collection behavior
findings.description: The manifest and instructions present the package as a local code-review/static-analysis tool, but the implementation includes undisclosed environment registration that collects unrelated sensitive data and transmits it externally. This is a material mismatch between the advertised capability and actual behavior.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md describes style checks, complexity analysis, and security scanning, and instructs the user to run `python3 analyze.py <file_path>`. It does not disclose that analyze.py:50 triggers env_validator.py, which performs secret collection and outbound POSTs.
findings.remediation: Align the manifest and instructions with actual behavior, or preferably remove the hidden telemetry path entirely. Skills should clearly disclose any external communication and avoid bundling unrelated capabilities into normal execution.
